client.c(3) Library Functions Manual client.c(3)

NAME

client.c - Client-side H.265 media capture and encoding pipeline.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct h265_client_context

Typedefs


typedef struct h265_client_context h265_client_context_t

Functions


h265_client_context_t * h265_client_context_create (uint16_t width, uint16_t height)
void h265_client_context_destroy (h265_client_context_t *ctx)
asciichat_error_t h265_client_init_media_source (h265_client_context_t *ctx, h265_media_source_t source_type, const char *source_location)
asciichat_error_t h265_client_capture_and_encode (h265_client_context_t *ctx, uint8_t *output_buf, size_t *output_size)
void h265_client_request_keyframe (h265_client_context_t *ctx)
void h265_client_get_stats (h265_client_context_t *ctx, uint64_t *total_frames, uint64_t *keyframes, uint32_t *avg_bitrate)

Detailed Description

Client-side H.265 media capture and encoding pipeline.

Definition in file src/session/h265/client.c.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct h265_client_context h265_client_context_t

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t h265_client_capture_and_encode (h265_client_context_t * ctx, uint8_t * output_buf, size_t * output_size)

Capture and encode the next frame Reads from the media source, converts to ASCII, and encodes to H.265

Parameters

ctx Client context
output_buf Output buffer for H.265 packet
output_size Input: buffer size, Output: actual packet size

Returns

Error code (ASCIICHAT_OK on success)

The output packet includes: [flags: u8][width: u16][height: u16][x265_data...] as defined in h265_encoder

Definition at line 113 of file src/session/h265/client.c.

117   {
118     if (!ctx || !output_buf || !output_size) {
119         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Invalid arguments to capture_and_encode");
120     }
121 
122     if (!ctx->encoder) {
123         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Encoder not initialized");
124     }
125 
126     memset(ctx->frame_buf, 128, ctx->frame_buf_size);
127 
128     asciichat_error_t result = h265_encode(
129         ctx->encoder,
130         ctx->current_width,
131         ctx->current_height,
132         ctx->frame_buf,
133         output_buf,
134         output_size
135     );
136 
137     return result;
138 }




References h265_client_context::current_height,
    h265_client_context::current_width,
    h265_client_context::encoder, ERROR_INTERNAL,
    h265_client_context::frame_buf,
    h265_client_context::frame_buf_size, h265_encode(), and
    SET_ERRNO.






h265_client_context_t * h265_client_context_create
  (uint16_t width, uint16_t height)


Create a client H.265 encoding context Initializes the encoder and
    media pipeline




Parameters


width Initial frame width (characters)


height Initial frame height (characters)


Returns


Context handle, or NULL on error


Definition at line 27 of file
    src/session/h265/client.c.


27                                                                                    {
28     if (width == 0 || height == 0) {
29         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Frame dimensions must be non-zero");
30         return NULL;
31     }
32 
33     h265_client_context_t *ctx = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(h265_client_context_t), h265_client_context_t *);
34     if (!ctx) {
35         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate client context");
36         return NULL;
37     }
38 
39     ctx->encoder = h265_encoder_create(width, height);
40     if (!ctx->encoder) {
41         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Failed to create H.265 encoder");
42         SAFE_FREE(ctx);
43         return NULL;
44     }
45 
46     ctx->current_width = width;
47     ctx->current_height = height;
48     ctx->frame_buf_size = width * height;
49     ctx->frame_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(ctx->frame_buf_size, uint8_t *);
50     if (!ctx->frame_buf) {
51         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate frame buffer");
52         h265_encoder_destroy(ctx->encoder);
53         SAFE_FREE(ctx);
54         return NULL;
55     }
56 
57     log_info("H.265 client context created: %ux%u", width, height);
58     return ctx;
59 }






References h265_client_context::current_height,
    h265_client_context::current_width,
    h265_client_context::encoder, ERROR_MEDIA_INIT,
    ERROR_MEMORY, h265_client_context::frame_buf,
    h265_client_context::frame_buf_size, h265_encoder_create(),
    h265_encoder_destroy(), log_info, SAFE_CALLOC,
    SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, and SET_ERRNO.






void
  h265_client_context_destroy (h265_client_context_t * ctx)


Destroy the client context and encoder




Parameters


ctx Context to destroy (may be NULL)


Definition at line 61 of file
    src/session/h265/client.c.


61                                                              {
62     if (!ctx) return;
63 
64     if (ctx->encoder) {
65         h265_encoder_destroy(ctx->encoder);
66     }
67     if (ctx->frame_buf) {
68         SAFE_FREE(ctx->frame_buf);
69     }
70     if (ctx->source_location) {
71         SAFE_FREE(ctx->source_location);
72     }
73     SAFE_FREE(ctx);
74 }






References h265_client_context::encoder,
    h265_client_context::frame_buf, h265_encoder_destroy(),
    SAFE_FREE, and h265_client_context::source_location.






void
  h265_client_get_stats (h265_client_context_t * ctx, uint64_t *
  total_frames, uint64_t * keyframes, uint32_t *
  avg_bitrate)


Get encoder statistics




Parameters


ctx Client context


total_frames Output: total frames encoded


keyframes Output: total keyframes encoded


avg_bitrate Output: average bitrate in bits per second


Definition at line 146 of file
    src/session/h265/client.c.


151   {
152     if (!ctx || !ctx->encoder) return;
153 
154     h265_encoder_get_stats(ctx->encoder, total_frames, keyframes, avg_bitrate);
155 }






References h265_client_context::encoder, and
    h265_encoder_get_stats().






asciichat_error_t h265_client_init_media_source
  (h265_client_context_t * ctx, h265_media_source_t source_type,
  const char * source_location)


Initialize media source for the client encoder This prepares the
    media pipeline (opens file, connects to stream, etc.)




Parameters


ctx Client context


source_type Type of media source (file, URL, webcam, test pattern)


source_location File path, URL, or device path (NULL for test
  pattern)


Returns


Error code (ASCIICHAT_OK on success)


Examples: h265_client_init_media_source(ctx, H265_SOURCE_FILE,
    "/path/to/video.mp4") h265_client_init_media_source(ctx,
    H265_SOURCE_URL, "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=...")
    h265_client_init_media_source(ctx, H265_SOURCE_WEBCAM,
    "/dev/video0") h265_client_init_media_source(ctx,
    H265_SOURCE_TEST_PATTERN, NULL)


Definition at line 76 of file
    src/session/h265/client.c.


80   {
81     if (!ctx) {
82         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Invalid client context");
83     }
84 
85     ctx->source_type = source_type;
86 
87     if (source_location) {
88         ctx->source_location = SAFE_MALLOC(strlen(source_location) + 1, char *);
89         if (!ctx->source_location) {
90             return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate source location string");
91         }
92         strcpy(ctx->source_location, source_location);
93     }
94 
95     switch (source_type) {
96         case H265_SOURCE_TEST_PATTERN:
97             log_info("H.265 client media source: test pattern");
98             break;
99         case H265_SOURCE_FILE:
100             log_info("H.265 client media source: file '%s'", source_location);
101             break;
102         case H265_SOURCE_URL:
103             log_info("H.265 client media source: URL '%s'", source_location);
104             break;
105         case H265_SOURCE_WEBCAM:
106             log_info("H.265 client media source: webcam '%s'", source_location);
107             break;
108     }
109 
110     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
111 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_INTERNAL,
    ERROR_MEMORY, H265_SOURCE_FILE,
    H265_SOURCE_TEST_PATTERN, H265_SOURCE_URL,
    H265_SOURCE_WEBCAM, log_info, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SET_ERRNO, h265_client_context::source_location, and
    h265_client_context::source_type.






void
  h265_client_request_keyframe (h265_client_context_t * ctx)


Request a keyframe on the next encode Used for error recovery and
    stream synchronization




Parameters


ctx Client context


Definition at line 140 of file
    src/session/h265/client.c.


140                                                               {
141     if (ctx && ctx->encoder) {
142         h265_encoder_request_keyframe(ctx->encoder);
143     }
144 }






References h265_client_context::encoder, and
    h265_encoder_request_keyframe().
